highlights.text: Back on Friday, I warned readers this was 6783, stating: “ My lean is we work up it to 6732, 6744, then 6766/83.” This could not worked out better and right off the open Sunday we began the trek upwards, hitting 6783 and we spent Monday and Tuesday sitting there.
I wrote yesterday: “Bear case tomorrow: On a short-term basis though, begins below 6756.” Price made its decision this morning, we lost 6756 and sold down to 6698 support.
My core setup spots it: The Failed Breakdown.
Institutions accumulate by waiting for a headline to hit (or engineering a sell themselves), which produces what I call an elevator down sell which causes price to flush a major low.
I wrote on Thursday at 4pm: “Obvious setup here the recovery of Tuesday’s 6593 daily low.” Early Friday morning, ES held 6572, recovered 6593, then ripped all day.
Failed breakdowns tend to magnetize towards major pattern resistances, and this is exactly what we saw.
This was a headline driven, elevator down sell.
If we are really melting down, the best setup here is to wait for the Failed Breakdown of yesterday’s 6694 daily low.
We flushed 6698 down to 6690, recovered/Failed Breakdown, ripped into the close.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive review into a power Failed Breakdown we had on Sunday shortly after the open at 6701 that gave us an 80 point rally Monday/Tuesday (this was given to readers in advance).

post_title: After a 200 Point Rally, SPX Sold Today. It Was Bought, But Can Bulls Sustain It Though? Oct 23 Plan
